Name: Gnome Sort
Author: Hamid Sarbazi-Azad
Description: Gnome sort, originally proposed as stupid sort, is a sorting algorithm which is similar to insertion sort in that it works with one item at a time but gets the item to the proper place by a series of swaps, similar to a bubble sort. It is conceptually simple, requiring no nested loops. The name comes from the behavior of a Dutch garden gnome sorting a line of flower pots.
